canonical_question: What does PSD2 and EU Open Banking require — SCA, API obligations, TPP licensing?
aliases: PSD2 compliance requirements for payment service providers
EU Open Banking API obligations under PSD2
Strong Customer Authentication SCA requirements Europe
constraints: EEA only — UK has diverged post-Brexit under its own PSRs 2017
PSD3/PSR provisional agreement Nov 2025, 21-month transition from final adoption
SCA exemptions are issuer-granted, not merchant-granted
TPPs require NCA authorization + eIDAS certificates
Interacts with GDPR, eIDAS, AML Directive, MiCA

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about UK Open Banking post-Brexit
User is asking solely about crypto-asset payment services

depends_on.label: GDPR — data protection framework constraining payment data processing

related_to.label: Cross-border data transfers — relevant for payment data outside EEA

often_confused_with.label: GDPR — separate regulation, PSD2 consent is not GDPR consent

inputs_needed.question: What is the entity's role in the payment chain?
Is the query about SCA implementation, exemptions, or both?
Does the user need information about upcoming PSD3/PSR changes?

applies_to.user_segment: Payment service providers, banks, fintechs, TPPs operating in or serving EU customers
applies_to.context: Any entity providing payment services, account information services, or payment initiation services within the EEA
